summary: Trading bot for ICP/ckUSDT pair on ICDex, written in Motoko. Version 3 in production with real placeOrder calls using fixed 2 ICP per order. Now implementing cancelOrder() in the trading loop (AR-2): each cycle will fetch open orders from ICDex and cancel them before placing new grid orders.
architectural_notes: Bot is a Motoko actor canister targeting ICDex pair canister jgxow-pqaaa-aaaar-qahaq-cai (ICP/ckUSDT)
Grid strategy: 20 orders (10 buy + 10 sell), spread of 45 bps (0.45%), mid-price derived from best bid/ask
Trading loop runs every 60 seconds using Timer.recurringTimer
Tokens are deposited directly into the bot canister (no external wallet allowance needed)
Grid repositioning cancels only orders outside the new grid range (not all open orders) to minimize fees
Order quantity is fixed at 2 ICP per order (both BUY and SELL sides), not calculated dynamically by dollar value

existing_features.title: Build a dashboard frontend that connects to the backend actor and displays: (1) a Bot Control panel with Start Bot and Stop Bot buttons that call startBot() and stopBot(), and a live status indicator (Running / Stopped); (2) a Configuration panel showing and allowing edits to intervalSeconds, spreadBps, and numOrders with a Save Config button; (3) a Market Data panel showing the last computed mid-price; (4) a Grid Preview table listing all computed orders with columns Side (BUY/SELL), Price, and Index. All data should be polled via React Query every 10 seconds.

Apply a dark, terminal-inspired visual theme across the entire frontend: dark charcoal/near-black backgrounds, monospace or semi-monospace typography, neon green accents for buy/active states, red accents for sell/stopped states, and amber/gold for neutral data. Use a clean grid-based layout with card panels, subtle borders, and minimal chrome. The aesthetic should feel like a professional trading terminal.

Add a Canister Balances panel to the dashboard frontend that displays the current ICP and ckUSDT balances held by the bot canister. Poll balances every 10 seconds using React Query hooks consistent with the existing useQueries.ts pattern. Format balances with appropriate decimal places (8 decimals for ICP, 6 for ckUSDT). Display a deposit instruction note reminding the user to send tokens directly to the canister principal.

new_feature_requests.summary: Activate real order placement via placeOrder()
Activate real order cancellation via cancelOrder()
Calculate order quantity based on $10 per order
Automatic grid repositioning on each trading loop cycle

Implement the backend Motoko actor for the ICDex grid trading bot. The actor must: (1) define the ICDex types (Side, OrderType, OrderId, OrderArgs, Level, Level10, Ticker); (2) reference the ICDex ICP/ckUSDT pair canister at principal 'jgxow-pqaaa-aaaar-qahaq-cai' with methods placeOrder, cancelOrder, getLevel10, and ticker; (3) expose stable configuration variables: intervalSeconds (default 60), spreadBps (default 45), numOrders (default 20); (4) expose public functions startBot() and stopBot() that start and cancel a recurring timer; (5) implement a private tradingLoop() that reads the Level10 order book, calculates mid-price as (bestBid + bestAsk) / 2, builds a symmetric grid of numOrders orders using the spreadBps offset, and logs each computed order price and side via Debug.print; (6) do NOT call placeOrder() or cancelOrder() in this phase — only log the computed orders.

Expose backend query/update endpoints that the frontend can call: getBotStatus() returning whether the bot is running (bool), getConfig() returning {intervalSeconds, spreadBps, numOrders}, and setConfig(intervalSeconds, spreadBps, numOrders) to update configuration. Also expose getLastMidPrice() returning the most recently computed mid-price (Nat), and getLastGrid() returning the last computed grid as an array of {side: Text, price: Nat} records so the frontend can display them without relying solely on Debug.print logs.

Implement token balance management in the backend actor: the bot must operate exclusively using ICP and ckUSDT tokens deposited directly into the canister's own account. Add query functions `getIcpBalance()` and `getCkUsdtBalance()` that return the canister's current token balances by calling the respective ICRC-1/ICP ledger canisters.

Activate real order placement in the trading loop: replace all Debug.print stubs with actual `placeOrder()` calls to the ICDex canister at `jgxow-pqaaa-aaaar-qahaq-cai`. Each order quantity must be calculated dynamically as 10 / midPrice (representing $10 per order in ckUSDT terms), using the current mid-price. Place all orders in the newly computed grid that do not already exist as open orders.

Implement smart grid repositioning: on each trading loop cycle, fetch the canister's currently open orders from ICDex, compare them against the newly calculated grid, and cancel only the orders whose price levels fall outside the new grid range. Orders whose prices still match a valid grid level must be left open to avoid unnecessary cancellation fees.

Expose backend query endpoints for canister token balances so the frontend can display deposited ICP and ckUSDT balances. Add `getIcpBalance()` and `getCkUsdtBalance()` as public query or update functions returning Nat values.

In the backend trading loop (tradingLoop()), replace all Debug.print stubs for order placement with actual placeOrder() calls to the ICDex canister at 'jgxow-pqaaa-aaaar-qahaq-cai'. Each order (both BUY and SELL) must use a fixed quantity of 2 ICP (represented as 200_000_000 e8s). Place all orders in the newly computed grid. Do not implement cancelOrder or grid repositioning in this step.

In the backend trading loop (tradingLoop()), before placing new grid orders each cycle, fetch all currently open orders from the ICDex canister at 'jgxow-pqaaa-aaaar-qahaq-cai' and call cancelOrder() on each of them. This ensures no stale orders accumulate across cycles. Do not implement smart grid repositioning or order comparison logic in this step — cancel all open orders unconditionally before placing the new grid.
